headline: Macron says 'everything' must be done to prevent new war in Lebanon
description: The French president said that Lebanese authorities had given him 'their commitment' to take control of Hezbollah positions, as France dispatches humanitarian aid.

headline: Kurdish factions could weaken Iran's regime from within
description: As US-Israeli strikes continue, attention is turning to Iran's Kurdish units, now united in an unprecedented coalition. But the Kurds' history, marked by fragile alliances and betrayals, fuels both hope and caution.

headline: Israel bombs Beirut after telling 700,000 residents to leave
description: The evacuation order gave residents of the Lebanese capital's southern suburbs just hours to leave their homes.

headline: How Israel and the US are waging their most intense air war in decades on Iran
description: The coordinated US-Israeli forces have devised 'unprecedented' operations to establish their military dominance over Iran, destroy its missile launchers and factories and gain control of their adversary's airspace.

headline: Nepal's youth, long forced abroad for work, look to elections for fresh start
description: Six months after the Gen Z uprising that brought down the government, many young Nepalis hope the March 5 legislative elections will give the country, plagued by unemployment, new impetus. More than 2,000 young people leave the country every day.

How Iran secretly acquired facial recognition technology through a Russian company
description: An investigation coordinated by Forbidden Stories, in partnership with Le Monde, reveals that an Iranian company purchased and then resold the Russian surveillance software FindFace to various entities within the regime.

headline: In Dubai, French influencers experience new reality under Iranian strikes
description: Many French social media personalities have chosen to relocate to the Emirati city, often for tax reasons. With Iranian bombs falling, online influencers like Maeva Ghennam are finding the dream less appealing.

headline: Iran's neighbors, under drone attacks, confront their vulnerability
description: Lacking experience in countering kamikaze drones, especially the infamous Shahed models, the Gulf countries have shown how unprepared they were for the threat posed by Iran, which is determined to exploit their vulnerability.
